DEFENDANT SATISFIED HIS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFFS' LAWSUIT IS TIME-BARRED AND PLAINTIFFS DID NOT PRESENT EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

Appearances:
Peter L. Weber, Esq., from Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, 415-788-1900, for Defendant and moving party
John R. Browne III, Esq., Law Offices, 415-421-6700, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANT MARIO G. PAOLINI, JR.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Granted. Defendant satisfied his burden of production that plaintiffs' lawsuit is time-barred and plaintiffs did not present evidence creating a triable issue. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Off Calendar Jan-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

DEFENDANT MARIO PAOLINI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS CAUSE OF ACTION 2 ; OVERRULED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1. THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S IMPROPER DECLARATION, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 301/PJB
